_meta.contract_ref: CONTRACT.md §5 -- key debates, not a laundry list; each argued causally and quantified; evidence quality graded honestly
_meta.note: Bear reads the crux (wallet-share of semiconductor R&D) as flat-to-compressing, not expanding. The central argument is not that the EDA moat breaks -- it is that the multiple embedded at $455.51 (26.4x FY2027 non-GAAP EPS) requires a re-rating toward 32x that depends on AI monetization evidence and Ansys synergy confirmation neither of which will arrive in the next 12 months.

bear_thesis: SNPS at $455.51 trades at 26.4x FY2027 non-GAAP EPS -- a multiple that will not expand to the 32x Daedalus targets because the three catalysts required for re-rating (agentic AI consumption revenue, Ansys revenue synergies, IP royalty ramp) are each 12-24 months from producing measurable numbers. That is the surface argument. Underneath it lies a harder structural problem: SNPS's own agentic AI product claims -- 2-5x productivity gains at Converge 2026 -- are the direct mechanism through which its seat-based EDA revenue compresses, and the consumption-pricing model that is supposed to offset this has zero contracted revenue at scale, 20 evaluation customers, and faces active resistance from hyperscaler procurement teams trained to minimize metered billing. Simultaneously, the IP segment is entering a deliberate revenue hole: management is transitioning hyperscalers from upfront IP license fees to royalty payments, which means FY2027 IP revenue will be below the trend line before royalties ramp in FY2028 -- a recognized $100-200M near-term headwind Theia flags but the base case paths do not fully reflect. Add China revenue structurally declining from 11.5% (FY2025) to 10% (Q2 FY2026) and continuing toward 8% or below as Empyrean captures trailing-edge design starts, and you have three simultaneous headwinds landing in FY2027 -- the year the stock is priced to prove the re-rating case. Any two of these firing in the same guidance cycle causes a guidance cut and a multiple re-rating to 22-24x, which maps to $380-390 on $17.25 EPS: the bear destination is already in the base intrinsic value ($390.86), not some catastrophe. The stock falls not because SNPS breaks but because a $455 entry price embeds assumptions that will not be confirmed for at least 24 months.
crux_reading: The crux is SNPS wallet-share of the global semiconductor R&D pool. The base reads this as expanding from 13.5% to 15% by FY2030 as agentic AI adds a consumption layer on top of seats. The bear reads it as FLAT TO COMPRESSING: wallet-share stays at 13.5% or slips to 11-12% because (a) the consumption pricing model faces the same budget-constrained procurement dynamic that has held wallet-share at 13-15% for 20 years -- chip companies have never voluntarily grown EDA spend faster than chip R&D, and the agentic AI era gives them a reason to shrink it (more output per dollar of EDA spend is a COST SAVINGS OPPORTUNITY, not a billing expansion); (b) IP royalty transition creates a one-time revenue recognition gap that makes FY2027 look worse than the trend line; and (c) China represents 10% of today's revenue and is structurally declining. The bear is not a collapse scenario -- it is a flat-revenue scenario at $10.5-10.8B by FY2030 vs the base $14.7B, which at compressed multiples (justified when growth stalls) values SNPS at $218-270.

pv_computation.interim_FCF_note: Bear interim FCF is lower than base due to compressed margins; ~$5.0B estimated across all segments years 1-3

scenario_spec.note: Exit multiples are compressed vs Daedalus base (EDA 21x vs 22.4x base; IP 15x vs 17.6x base; Ansys 16x vs 19.5x base) because a flat-revenue trajectory does not support the same Gordon exit multiple as a growing one. EDA terminal growth collapses from 4.5% to 3.5% in the bear (g_lt = pool growth 3.8% x flat wallet-share, rounded), reducing the Gordon multiple from 22.4x to 21.0x. IP bear g_lt falls to 2.5% (flat wallet-share + China impairment), Gordon multiple from 17.6x to ~15x. Ansys bear reflects integration failure: terminal growth 3.5% (no cross-sell), ROIC same 40%, g/r/ROIC gives ~13x Gordon, but floored at 16x reflecting Ansys's standalone monopoly value in engineering simulation (it would be worth 16x standalone at stagnant growth). Sources: Theia bear scenario parameters; Daedalus valuation.json bear scenario confirms $218.

key_debates.title: AI self-disruption: SNPS's own product claims make seat compression certain; the question is whether consumption pricing offsets it before FY2030
key_debates.break_mechanism: SNPS told the market at Converge 2026 that Agent Engineer achieves 2-5x productivity gains. This is not a vague future promise -- it is management's public characterization of a product already in evaluation at 20 customer sites. If a chip design team gets 2-5x more output per engineer, the rational customer response is to shrink the engineering team, not the EDA budget. The mechanism: the customer captures the productivity gain as headcount savings, and the EDA vendor captures only the share it can extract through the consumption pricing transition. That transition requires (a) the customer to accept a hybrid subscription-plus-consumption model that did not previously exist, (b) SNPS to meter and bill AI agent compute usage, and (c) no cap or ceiling in the enterprise license agreement (hyperscalers specifically negotiate ELAs with consumption caps because the alternative -- an open meter -- introduces budget uncertainty CFOs will not tolerate). The base case assumes this transition works and wallet-share expands from 13.5% to 15% by FY2030. The bear argues it does not: customers negotiate ELA consumption caps at or near current spend levels, seat counts shrink 20-30% over 3 years (from 2-5x productivity), and the net effect is flat-to-declining EDA segment revenue by FY2028-2029. The 20-year history of EDA wallet-share at 13-15% is the precedent: every prior cycle of tool productivity improvement (place-and-route automation in the 1990s, formal verification in the 2000s, high-level synthesis in the 2010s) made engineers more productive, yet wallet-share never expanded because chip companies always captured the cost savings. There is no reason to expect a different outcome unless SNPS can impose metered billing at scale -- and the evidence that it can is 20 evaluation customers with no disclosed revenue.
key_debates.observable_evidence: SNPS backlog declined $400M in two quarters: $11.4B (October 2025) to $11.3B (January 2026) to $11.0B (April 2026). Backlog is the leading indicator of booked revenue that will recognize in the next 12-36 months. A declining backlog while revenue is growing means new contract bookings are running BELOW revenue recognition -- the pipeline is thinning. This is precisely what you would expect if customers are signing shorter-duration contracts (waiting to see the AI/consumption model before committing to multi-year deals) rather than renewing the traditional 3-year EDA subscription. Design Automation segment Q2 FY2026: the backlog decline is not driven by IP (which was already weak) but is a combined-entity indicator. Citi's post-earnings note (March 2026 IR meeting) specifically flagged that 'backlog lags peers' -- Cadence has not reported a comparable backlog decline. Stock fell 2% after a 6.7% EPS beat and guidance raise on May 27 2026: the market is telling you the EPS number is not the debate -- it is the sustainability of the EDA renewal rate as AI productivity transforms the tool.
key_debates.historical_precedent: CAD software in mechanical engineering from 2005-2015: SolidWorks and AutoCAD each delivered major productivity improvements (parametric design, simulation integration). Over that decade, large engineering firms reduced design engineer headcount 15-20% at steady-state program sizes. Dassault Systemes and Autodesk both experienced 5-8% annual revenue growth in that period vs 12-15% in the prior decade -- the market grew but at half its prior rate as productivity improvements were captured by customers. The EDA parallel is tighter: EDA is a duopoly vs the CAD market's oligopoly, giving SNPS more pricing power, but hyperscaler EDA customers are more sophisticated than manufacturing firms and their procurement leverage is stronger. The period from FY2028-FY2031 maps to the 2008-2013 window in CAD when the productivity gains were fully manifest but the pricing model had not yet shifted. Autodesk's subscription transition (FY2016-FY2018) caused three years of flat revenue: total software revenue was $2.1B in FY2016, $1.99B in FY2017, $2.16B in FY2018 before recovering to $3.3B in FY2020. Adobe Creative Cloud (FY2013): subscribers declined for two quarters, total revenue fell 8% in the transition year before recovering. Both took longer than the companies initially guided. SNPS is attempting a harder version of this (subscription-to-subscription-plus-consumption vs subscription-to-pure-subscription), with hyperscaler customers who have more leverage than individual designers.
key_debates.dollar_per_share_downside: $97 downside from EDA segment bear vs base (Daedalus valuation.json: base EDA EV $49.3B vs bear EDA EV approximately $30.7B = $18.6B difference / 0.191B shares = $97/share from EDA alone). In the 12-month framing: if Q3 or Q4 FY2026 earnings show (a) backlog declines a third quarter in a row OR (b) FY2027 guidance implies EDA growth below 7% (vs the base's 9.5% CAGR), the stock re-rates from 26.4x to 22-23x FY2027 EPS. At $17.25 EPS and 22x: $379/share. That is a $76/share decline from current $455.51 (-16.7%) from the multiple compression alone, before any EPS reduction. If FY2027 EDA revenue guidance comes in at $5.1B rather than the $5.4B base, NOPAT is $2.05B vs $2.05B base -- essentially unchanged at FY2027. The damage is in the out-years and the multiple. Total quantified bear downside from C1 alone: $76-97/share (multiple compression + partial EDA revenue miss).
key_debates.evidence_grade_explanation: The seat compression mechanism is real and named (SNPS's own productivity claims are the trigger). The backlog trend is observable and recent ($400M decline in 6 months, Theia demand.json monitoring_variables). However, the consumption pricing resistance by hyperscaler EDA customers is a mechanism that has not yet fired -- no ELA renegotiation or cap announcement has been disclosed. The backlog decline is early signal, not proof. Graded MECHANISM-NOT-YET-FIRING: real, named, unfired. An OBSERVABLE-AND-NEAR grade would require a disclosed customer refusing the consumption model or a segment revenue miss attributable to fewer seats. Sources: SNPS_crux.json R4 (seat compression, 25-35% probability by FY2030); SNPS_demand.json bear EDA path ($5.50B FY2030, 2.7% CAGR); SNPS_market_facts.json backlog data.
key_debates.what_proves_me_wrong: Two consecutive quarters of backlog growth (return to $11.4B or above) plus a quantified disclosure of consumption-based revenue bookings in Q3 or Q4 FY2026 earnings (not just 'we have 20 evaluation customers' but 'we booked $Xm in consumption revenue in the quarter'). Either one shifts this to a bull signal. Both together would indicate the consumption model is working and the seat-compression risk is being captured by pricing rather than being surrendered as headcount savings.

key_debates.title: IP royalty model transition creates a mandatory FY2027 revenue hole that hits guidance in the next 12 months
key_debates.break_mechanism: Management explicitly guided that the hyperscaler IP royalty model transition completes 'near end FY2026' (October 2026 fiscal year-end). Under the prior model, hyperscalers signed multi-year upfront license agreements: SNPS books the revenue on signing. Under the new royalty model, SNPS earns a per-chip royalty only as silicon ships. The hardware design cycle is 18-24 months. A chip design that starts in Q4 FY2026 does not tape out until Q2-Q4 FY2027 at the earliest. Royalty revenue on that design does not arrive until the chip ships in volume, which is H2 FY2027 at the earliest and more realistically FY2028. The bridge: $300-400M of IP revenue that HISTORICALLY would have been recognized upfront in FY2026 and FY2027 will instead recognize on a drip in FY2027-FY2028 as chips ship. The magnitude -- Theia explicitly flags '$100-200M one-time headwind in FY2027' in SNPS_demand.json (IP bear path FY2027 annotation). The IP bear path already runs at $1.90B in FY2027 vs the base $2.20B -- a $300M gap that is LARGELY attributable to this recognition timing effect, not a structural revenue loss. But the stock market does not distinguish a recognition timing miss from a structural miss in the quarter it happens. Confirmation: management's own Q2 FY2026 guidance for Design IP was that it is 'down 6% YoY' in Q2 and recovers in H2 FY2026. If the H2 recovery undershoot because the royalty transition is pulling more revenue forward into timing gaps than expected, Q4 FY2026 and Q1 FY2027 could both disappoint. This is the event that causes a guidance cut and triggers the multiple to stay at 26x rather than re-rate to 32x.
key_debates.observable_evidence: Design IP was down 6% year-over-year in Q2 FY2026 (SNPS_market_facts.json key_financial_facts.fq2_2026_results.design_ip_yoy_pct = -6.0%), even as Q2 showed a 12% sequential recovery from Q1's trough. The segment is recovering from the May-July 2025 export restriction episode, but is NOT recovering to its FY2024 level of $1.98B annualized. The H2 FY2026 recovery is management's guidance, not a contracted fact. Separately, the royalty model transition is management's explicit statement: Goldman Sachs's May 2026 pre-earnings note specifically called out 'agentic AI pricing debate as the key forward catalyst' and Citi's March 2026 IR meeting notes cite royalty-based IP monetization as an 'upside driver' -- implying neither firm has modeled it into FY2027 estimates. A FY2027 IP miss is not priced into the $17.25 EPS consensus. The $17.25 FY2027 EPS guidance from management (first disclosed May 27 2026) already has considerable execution risk embedded in it. A $200M IP revenue shortfall at 22% margin = $44M NOPAT reduction; at 30x multiplied through = $1.3B equity value reduction = $7/share direct. But if this causes a FY2027 EPS guidance cut from $17.25 to $16.25 (a $1 miss), the stock at 26.4x current multiple falls to 26.4 x $16.25 = $429, a $26/share decline from current $455.
key_debates.historical_precedent: The ARM royalty model transition from the 2000s to 2010s is the closest analog. ARM transitioned customers from upfront architecture licenses to royalty streams over several years in the early 2000s. ARM's revenue was lumpy and below analyst expectations for 3-4 years during the transition, despite the royalty model ultimately proving more valuable. The stock underperformed during the transition. A more direct analog: Arm Holdings (post-NVIDIA attempted acquisition, 2021-2023) saw its IP segment revenues flat to declining during contract renegotiations as customers pushed back on new royalty terms. SNPS is attempting the same transition but with hyperscalers (Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Meta) who are specifically structured to avoid royalty-bearing IP relationships -- Amazon built custom Graviton/Trainium silicon SPECIFICALLY to reduce ARM royalty exposure. The same logic applies to SNPS interface IP: if hyperscalers see SNPS transitioning to royalties, they will accelerate R&D into alternative interface IP sources to reduce royalty dependence. This is not a tail event -- it is the commercial incentive structure of the customer base.
key_debates.dollar_per_share_downside: Near-term (12-month): if FY2027 IP revenue comes in at $1.90B (Theia IP bear path) vs base $2.20B, the $300M revenue shortfall at 22% bear margin = $66M NOPAT reduction. Direct multiple impact: $66M x 17.6x IP exit multiple = $1.16B equity value reduction = $6/share direct. But the guidance cut effect is larger: a $1 EPS reduction in FY2027 guidance (from $17.25 to $16.25, achievable with a $200M IP miss plus margin pressure) at 26.4x current multiple = $26/share decline. Full bear IP scenario (FY2030 IP at $2.20B vs base $3.38B): Daedalus base IP EV = $12.1B vs bear IP EV = $6.3B (from valuation.json bear segment_implied). Difference: $5.8B / 0.191B shares = $30/share from IP segment alone. Total quantified downside from C2: $26-30/share over a 12-18 month horizon.
key_debates.evidence_grade_explanation: Observable because the IP royalty transition is explicitly management-guided ('near end FY2026') and Design IP is already down 6% YoY in Q2 FY2026 with management calling it a trough. Near because the recognition gap will land in FY2027 guidance, which is disclosed at Q4 FY2026 earnings (around December 2026). The mechanism (upfront license revenue pulled into the new royalty model, creating a recognition gap before royalties ramp) is the DIRECT CONSEQUENCE of a confirmed management action, not a hypothetical. This is not a tail -- it is an announced transition with a known timing implication. Sources: SNPS_crux.json R5 (IP royalty model transition, 70-80% probability); SNPS_demand.json IP bear path FY2027 $1.90B and transition_headwind_note; SNPS_market_facts.json design_ip_yoy_pct -6.0%; SNPS_crux.json R5 magnitude '$15-30/share near-term overhang'.
key_debates.what_proves_me_wrong: IP segment Q3 FY2026 revenue grows to $480M+ (above the $454M Q2 level, confirming the sequential trend) AND Q4 FY2026 IP delivers $500M+ (above prior year Q4), AND management provides FY2027 IP guidance at or above $2.0B at Q4 FY2026 earnings (around December 2026). Three consecutive quarters of IP sequential growth plus a credible FY2027 guide would confirm the royalty transition is proceeding without a revenue hole.

key_debates.title: Ansys integration scale mismatch: this acquisition is 70x larger than any prior SNPS deal, and cross-sell requires bridging two permanently different buyer personas
key_debates.break_mechanism: SNPS's largest prior acquisition before Ansys was Magma Design Automation in 2012 at $507M. Ansys cost $35B. The ratio is 69x. Every prior SNPS integration involved acquiring EDA tools sold to IC designers within the same companies that already used SNPS EDA. Ansys sells to mechanical engineers, systems engineers, aerospace structural analysts, and automotive powertrain simulation teams. These buyers do not attend the same meetings as IC designers, report to different VPs, have different budget cycles, and evaluate software on different criteria (SNPS EDA customers evaluate on PPA optimization and tapeout success rate; Ansys customers evaluate on FEA accuracy and simulation throughput). The $400M revenue synergy target requires SNPS's EDA sales team to either (a) learn to sell Ansys products to mechanical engineers or (b) Ansys's sales team to expand into SNPS's EDA customers with a new simulation product (Multiphysics Fusion). Option (a) is asking EDA engineers to sell multiphysics FEA -- a fundamentally different sales motion. Option (b) is selling an EDA-company-branded simulation tool into accounts where Ansys already has deep relationships as a standalone vendor, now under a new parent. The risk: Dassault Systemes (SIMULIA), Hexagon (MSC Nastran), and Altair all observed SNPS pay $35B for Ansys. Each has launched an aggressive campaign to call on Ansys customers and offer 'pure-play simulation' alternatives without the EDA vendor overhead. The historical precedent is Siemens's acquisition of Mentor Graphics in 2017: Mentor's customers (EDA buyers) were initially loyal, but Mentor's market-share growth slowed materially post-acquisition as customers worried about vendor strategy alignment. SNPS has acknowledged this risk by calling Multiphysics Fusion a 'chip-to-system' product -- but chip-to-system is SNPS's framing, not the mechanical engineering buyer's framing. A structural simulation buyer thinks of Ansys as a standalone FEA/CFD tool, not as a chip design optimization add-on.
key_debates.observable_evidence: Ansys FY2026 revenue contribution guided at $2.9B (SNPS_market_facts.json ansys_integration). Annualizing Q2 FY2026 Ansys revenue of $652M: $652M x 4 = $2.608B. For the segment to hit $2.9B full year, H2 must deliver approximately $1.25B ($625M/quarter), consistent with seasonal uplift. The Q2 beat versus estimate ($652M vs $616M estimate) is encouraging. But the Multiphysics Fusion product only went GA on June 17, 2026 -- literally four days before this analysis. There are zero named customer wins, zero revenue contribution, and zero evidence of cross-sell at scale. The company describes beta customers as 'market leaders' -- they are unnamed. The $400M cost synergy target (50% by end FY2026, per management) is on track. But cost synergies and revenue synergies are different animals. Cost synergies require firing redundant employees and consolidating infrastructure: SNPS can control this. Revenue synergies require a customer who was not previously buying Ansys to buy it, or a customer who was not previously buying a SNPS-Ansys combined product to buy one: the customer controls this. Daedalus's base case gives Ansys $4.25B FY2030 revenue (11.3% CAGR from $2.77B FY2026). The bear path is $3.10B (2.9% CAGR). A 2.9% CAGR is below Ansys's standalone FY2024 growth of 12%, implying the acquisition DESTROYED growth relative to what Ansys would have achieved independently. The mechanism: Dassault aggressively poaching accounts post-acquisition, and Ansys customers uncertain about long-term product roadmap under SNPS ownership slowing their renewal commits.
key_debates.historical_precedent: Synopsys/Magma Design Automation (2012, $507M): integration took 2 years longer than the guided 18 months. Mentor/Siemens (2017): Mentor's EDA market share growth slowed from 3-4pp/year in the 2012-2016 period to 1-2pp/year post-acquisition as customers worried about industrial-conglomerate ownership. Parametric Technology Corporation (PTC) acquiring Servigistics (2004) and Arena Solutions (2021): cross-selling enterprise software from an engineering software company into maintenance and supply-chain buyers generated persistent revenue synergy underperformance. The closer analog is the Ansys-scale transaction: ANSYS itself acquired Spaceclaim in 2014 ($85M, small by comparison) and noted it took 3 years before cross-sell revenue contribution was material. The Spaceclaim acquisition required ANSYS to sell 3D CAD to FEA buyers -- the same persona. SNPS is attempting to sell FEA to IC design buyers and EDA to FEA buyers simultaneously, a two-directional cross-sell that has no direct precedent at this scale.
key_debates.dollar_per_share_downside: Daedalus base Ansys EV = $21.0B (valuation.json outputs segment Ansys implied_ev_b). Bear Ansys EV = $13.9B (valuation.json bear scenario segment_implied Ansys). Difference: $7.1B / 0.191B shares = $37/share from Ansys segment alone. In the 12-month framing: if FY2027 Ansys revenue guides to $2.9B (flat with FY2026) rather than the base's $3.10B, the miss of $200M at 30% bear margin = $53M NOPAT reduction; at 16x bear exit multiple = $848M equity reduction = $4.4/share direct. The multiple compression effect is larger: an Ansys synergy miss in FY2027 guidance that delays the $400M revenue synergy target from Year 4 to Year 6 reduces the Ansys EV by approximately $5B (the NPV of 2 years of $400M synergy at 10% WACC is ~$700M, and the market multiple applied to the synergy revenue is ~7x EV/revenue = $2.8B, plus the market de-rates the synergy confidence which compresses the overall Ansys exit multiple from 19.5x to 16x -- a further $5.6B reduction). Total Ansys-segment bear: $37/share vs base. In isolation on a 12-month horizon: $10-15/share if only the synergy timeline slips without a full multiple re-rate.
key_debates.evidence_grade_explanation: The cross-sell structural challenge (different buyer personas) is real and named. The precedent (EDA M&A integration delays, cross-domain software cross-sell underperformance) is documented. But the mechanism has not fired: Q2 FY2026 Ansys beat its estimate, and cost synergies are running ahead of schedule. The revenue synergy test arrives in FY2027 when Multiphysics Fusion needs to show customer wins and expanding ACV. The structural difficulty is observable in the product architecture and buyer-persona analysis; the revenue consequence has not yet manifested in reported numbers. Graded MECHANISM-NOT-YET-FIRING: the break mechanism is structurally sound, the precedents are clear, but Ansys revenue has not yet disappointed. Sources: SNPS_crux.json R1 (Ansys integration, 25-35% probability, $20-30/share downside); SNPS_valuation.json bear scenario Ansys EV $13.9B; Theia Ansys bear path (Magma/Mentor precedents; Dassault competition response).
key_debates.what_proves_me_wrong: Two consecutive quarters of Ansys revenue at or above $700M (quarterly run-rate consistent with management's $2.9B FY2026 target) PLUS a named cross-sell win for Multiphysics Fusion in an SNPS EDA customer account (any disclosure of a semiconductor company buying Multiphysics Fusion that was not previously an Ansys customer). The first confirms the base business is intact; the second confirms the cross-sell thesis is moving. Both together by Q1 FY2027 (January 2027 earnings) would substantially de-risk the Ansys integration bear.

key_debates.title: China structural decline is accelerating and provides the near-term catalyst that triggers the first FY2027 guidance cut
key_debates.break_mechanism: China was 15% of SNPS revenue historically, 11.54% in FY2025 (~$814M), and 10% in Q2 FY2026 (~$228M quarterly, ~$910M annualized). The trend is clear: each fiscal year brings a step-down. The mechanism is not primarily export controls (the May 2025 BIS restriction was lifted in July 2025) -- it is organic localization. Empyrean Technology (listed in China, $69M H1 2025 revenue, 73% R&D intensity per Theia) is capturing trailing-edge design starts for analog, memory, and legacy digital EDA. SNPS cannot address trailing-edge China without an export license risk every time the US-China technology relationship deteriorates. Chinese chip companies designing 28nm and older nodes for domestic consumption have a financially rational incentive to adopt Empyrean over SNPS: (a) no export control risk, (b) government subsidies for domestic EDA adoption, (c) the capability gap at trailing nodes is closing. The structural argument is not that SNPS loses 100% of China revenue -- it is that the steady-state China revenue settles at 6-8% of total (from the current 10-11% trajectory), representing a $200-300M permanent revenue reduction from peak China levels of $1.0B+ implied by the 15% historical share. The IP segment is more exposed than EDA: interface IP for China-designed chips carries explicit export license requirements under current BIS rules, and the Empyrean domestic IP alternative is expanding. A second episode of BIS export restrictions -- a tail event but not zero-probability given the ongoing US-China tech war -- would remove $800M+ in revenue and trigger an immediate guidance cut.
key_debates.observable_evidence: China revenue declining in each measurable period: 15% historically, 11.54% FY2025, ~10% Q2 FY2026 (SNPS_market_facts.json china_risk: 'China revenue guided slightly down YoY in FY2026'). The trend is three data points of sequential reduction. In absolute terms: $814M FY2025, implied ~$910M annualized from Q2 FY2026 -- NOT recovering despite the July 2025 export restriction lifting. Design IP was down 6% YoY in Q2 FY2026 (SNPS_market_facts.json design_ip_yoy_pct = -6.0%), and IP is the segment most exposed to China given interface IP requirements. Empyrean is growing: $69M H1 2025 revenue at 73% R&D intensity (SNPS_crux.json R3). A 73% R&D-to-revenue ratio means Empyrean is burning cash at a Chinese government-subsidized pace to build capability, not to produce profits. This is not commercial competition -- it is state-funded EDA displacement, which is categorically harder to compete against than commercial competition. Timeline to complete trailing-edge capture: Theia puts it at 3-5 years (SNPS_crux.json R3 timeline_to_global_threat), meaning the China revenue headwind is present and growing from today through FY2030, not a distant concern.
key_debates.historical_precedent: US semiconductor equipment companies (ASML, KLA, Lam Research) faced China export control episodic risk from 2018-2023. In each episode, the pattern was: restriction announced, stock falls 10-20%, restriction scope narrows or is eventually lifted, stock partially recovers. But each episode accelerated Chinese domestic investment in the restricted category (domestically-designed lithography at SMEE, domestic etching tools at NAURA). The EDA analog: the May-July 2025 BIS restriction was lifted, but SNPS China revenue has not recovered to pre-restriction levels -- it is settling lower as customers accelerated domestic alternative adoption during the restriction window. The Adobe China experience (2021-2023) is a parallel: Adobe was effectively blocked from expanding in China due to regulatory concerns, and China revenue declined from ~8% to ~5% of total over two years. SNPS is at 10% today on the way to 6-8% on current trends.
key_debates.dollar_per_share_downside: Partial organic shift scenario (60-70% probability per Theia R2): China declines from $910M current run-rate to $600M by FY2028 as trailing-edge designs shift to Empyrean. $310M revenue loss at 35% blended margin = $108M NOPAT reduction. At 20x blended exit multiple = $2.16B equity value reduction / 0.191B shares = $11/share direct. Full ban scenario (10-15% probability per Theia R2): China loses $814M at 40% IP/EDA margin = $326M NOPAT reduction. At 20x = $6.5B equity reduction / 0.191B = $34/share. But the scenario-weighted downside: (0.65 x $11) + (0.12 x $34) = $7.15 + $4.08 = $11.23/share scenario-weighted downside. This is not the biggest individual number -- but it has the highest probability and arrives in the next 12-24 months as organic localization continues. The risk is a near-term catalyst (a bad China quarter or a second BIS episode) that triggers a guidance cut and shifts the market narrative from 'China recovered' to 'China is structurally declining.' That narrative shift, not the revenue loss itself, is what drives the multiple from 26x to 22x. At 22x FY2027 EPS $17.25 = $379/share -- $76/share below current price.
key_debates.evidence_grade_explanation: Observable: China revenue declining in three consecutive measurement periods (15% historically, 11.54% FY2025, 10% Q2 FY2026). Empyrean growing at 73% R&D intensity (state-subsidized). Export control episode (May-July 2025) confirmed the binary tail risk is non-zero. Near: the organic localization trend is already in the revenue data and will manifest in each quarterly China revenue disclosure going forward. This is OBSERVABLE-AND-NEAR because the mechanism is currently active in the reported numbers, not a future hypothesis. The tail (full ban) is classified as TAIL separately -- the organic shift component is OBSERVABLE. Sources: SNPS_market_facts.json china_risk; SNPS_crux.json R2 (full ban $52/share, partial $20/share) and R3 (Empyrean 3-5 year trailing-edge capture timeline); SNPS_demand.json IP bear path (China declines to 5-6% of IP by FY2028).
key_debates.what_proves_me_wrong: China revenue stabilizes at 9-10% of total for two consecutive quarters (Q3 and Q4 FY2026) and IP segment China-related revenue returns to positive YoY growth by Q4 FY2026. Stabilization at 9-10% would indicate the organic localization effect is being offset by new Chinese chip design program growth (which is plausible as Chinese semiconductor investment grows, even if domestic EDA captures the trailing-edge). A return to positive YoY IP growth in China would suggest SNPS is retaining the higher-value leading-edge China design programs even as it loses trailing-edge work to Empyrean.

structural_forensic_check.sbc_quality: SBC $893M in FY2025 (SNPS_valuation.json earnings_bridge). At 191M shares, this is $4.67/share annually. SNPS is currently prioritizing debt repayment over buybacks (zero buybacks in FY2024 and FY2025 per EDGAR). SBC therefore represents REAL dilution with no buyback offset. The management-reported non-GAAP EPS of $14.76 adds SBC back; the OWNER FCF after SBC is $10.09/share (Daedalus's computation in valuation.json). At $455.51, cash P/E on owner FCF is 45.1x. This is an important signal: the headline 26.4x NTM P/E is the management-framed number; the SBC-inclusive cash P/E is 45x. The stock is priced for material future FCF growth to justify this multiple. Any scenario that delays that growth by 2+ years results in a prolonged period at 45x cash P/E that is indefensible for a name with decelerating revenue growth.
structural_forensic_check.goodwill_and_intangibles: Goodwill $26.9B + intangibles $12.7B = $39.6B of acquisition-related assets (Theia crux.json balance_sheet reference; market_facts.json key_financial_facts.balance_sheet). Total assets are heavily Ansys-goodwill. The risk: if Ansys underdelivers on revenue synergies and growth slows to 3% CAGR (bear case), management must eventually evaluate a goodwill impairment. A $35B acquisition that produces $3.1B FY2030 revenue at 30% margins = $0.82B NOPAT -- at 16x bear exit multiple this Ansys segment is worth $9.2B in equity terms vs $35B paid. The impairment math is severe in the full bear, though goodwill impairments typically lag the operating deterioration by 2-3 years (management tends to delay). A goodwill impairment would be a GAAP event only and would not affect non-GAAP EPS, but it would signal to the market that the acquisition destroyed value -- a sentiment event that would pressure the multiple regardless of non-GAAP metrics.
structural_forensic_check.backlog_quality: Backlog $11.0B as of April 2026, down from $11.4B in October 2025. $400M decline in 6 months. The bull dismisses this as noise or billing-cycle timing. The bear notes that declining backlog while total revenue is growing ($9.665B guide FY2026 vs $7.054B FY2025 -- though FY2025 only had 3.5 months of Ansys) is a signal that the ORDER BOOK is thinning relative to the revenue run-rate. The backlog covers 1.14x FY2026 annual revenue (11.0B / 9.665B). If this ratio continues declining, it means SNPS is consuming backlog faster than it is replenishing it -- a revenue sustainability concern for FY2027+. The bull requires new bookings to accelerate in FY2027 (driven by Multiphysics Fusion, consumption pricing). If the opposite occurs (bookings decelerate as customers adopt shorter-duration contracts or wait for the AI pricing model), backlog could fall below 1.0x FY2027 revenue by mid-FY2027 -- a threshold that historically triggers sell-side concern.
structural_forensic_check.cash_conversion: Daedalus FCF guide $2.0B FY2026 on $9.665B revenue = 20.7% FCF margin. Pre-Ansys, SNPS generated ~28-30% FCF margins. The dilution is entirely Ansys acquisition leverage: $10B debt at ~5% interest rate = $500M interest expense annually, net of 12% tax = $440M annual interest cost that did not exist pre-deal. The NOPAT-based SOTP (Daedalus) correctly capitalizes the unlevered value and then subtracts net debt -- this is methodologically correct. But the bear notes that $440M of annual interest expense that cannot be reduced until debt is paid down constrains capital return for 3-4 years. With FCF at $2.0B and $440M of interest cost built in, debt paydown of ~$1.5B/year is realistic. Net debt of $7.52B (as of June 2026) does not reach sub-$2B levels until FY2030 -- at which point SNPS can resume buybacks. Four years of no buybacks while SBC dilutes at $4.67/share annually (accumulating ~$18-19/share of dilution without offset) is the bear's capital return argument.
structural_forensic_check.verdict: No outright accounting red flag. The concerns are structural: SBC dilution without buyback offset is a real cost the bull papers over; goodwill impairment is a tail event in the full bear; backlog trajectory is an early warning signal that requires monitoring. The biggest quality concern is the gap between non-GAAP EPS ($14.76) and owner FCF per share after SBC ($10.09). The 46% premium of the reported metric to the owner metric is not fraud -- it is a structural consequence of a $35B acquisition with $893M SBC -- but it overstates the cash earnings power of the business and will constrain capital return for years.

floor_check: The $218 bear floor is defensible as a floor because it assumes ONLY the named bear mechanisms fire (AI seat compression offsets consumption pricing, China continues declining to 6% of revenue, Ansys cross-sell produces zero synergy revenue by FY2030) -- without assuming a catastrophic collapse of the core EDA moat. The $218 is computed on $5.50B FY2030 EDA revenue, which still represents SNPS growing EDA from $4.93B (FY2026E) to $5.50B over four years -- a 2.7% CAGR, not a revenue decline. The structural EDA duopoly (SNPS and CDNS with combined 60%+ market share, >$10B switching costs embedded in customer workflows) means the business never goes to zero. Even in the Autodesk subscription transition analogy (3 years of flat revenue), Autodesk did not lose customers -- it restructured economics with them. The floor of $218 is supported by the contracted backlog of $11.0B providing 12-month revenue certainty of ~$5.4B (49% of backlog per SNPS_crux.json contracted_vs_extrapolated), which means even if all new business disappears, FY2027 revenue cannot fall below ~$6B. That floor level at bear margins and compressed multiples gives an intrinsic value well above $100. The $218 floor therefore reflects a severe-but-not-catastrophic scenario, consistent with a probability-weighted 'all three bear mechanisms fire simultaneously' outcome.
near_term_downside_path: The path from $455.51 to below $387 (-15%) within 12 months does not require the full bear to materialize. It requires only two events: (1) Q3 FY2026 earnings (August 2026) or Q4 FY2026 earnings (December 2026) show Design IP Q3 revenue flat-to-declining sequentially despite management's H2 recovery guidance, confirming the royalty model transition is creating a larger-than-expected revenue gap (C2). This would force a FY2027 EPS guidance reduction at Q4 FY2026 earnings from $17.25 to ~$16.00-16.50. (2) Separately or simultaneously, the September 30 Investor Day fails to deliver a quantified agentic AI consumption revenue path (fails to announce contracted consumption revenue or named deals), leaving the stock at 26.4x FY2027 EPS with no re-rating catalyst confirmed. If FY2027 EPS guides to $16.25 and the market stays at 26.4x (no re-rating because the Investor Day disappointed on consumption revenue specifics): $16.25 x 26.4 = $429 -- a $26/share decline. If the IP miss also triggers the market to question FY2027 revenue guidance more broadly (Ansys is on track but IP disappoints), the multiple could compress slightly to 24x: $16.25 x 24 = $390 -- consistent with Daedalus's base intrinsic value of $390.86 but reached from the market price side via guidance cut plus multiple compression, not via fundamental improvement. This path is realistic, does not require China to worsen materially, does not require Ansys to disappoint, and does not require the full AI disruption scenario. It requires only the ONE event that is already guided to occur (royalty model transition completing near end FY2026) to play out with a larger revenue recognition gap than the consensus has modeled.
self_conviction_basis: The C2 debate (IP royalty transition revenue hole in FY2027) is the bear's highest-conviction argument: it is OBSERVABLE-AND-NEAR, the mechanism is explicitly guided by management, and the consensus ($17.25 FY2027 EPS) does not appear to fully model the recognition timing headwind. This alone makes the stock HOLD rather than BUY at $455. The C4 debate (China organic decline) is also OBSERVABLE with near-term manifestation, adding to the near-term cautionary case. The C1 debate (AI seat compression) is the bear's most important long-term argument but is MECHANISM-NOT-YET-FIRING, making it a 2028-2030 story rather than a 12-month catalyst. The C3 debate (Ansys scale mismatch) is also MECHANISM-NOT-YET-FIRING and requires 3-4 years to fully manifest. MEDIUM conviction because: (a) two of four debates are near-term and observable; (b) the core EDA moat is real and prevents a catastrophic collapse; (c) Elliott activism and the September 30 Investor Day are genuine re-rating catalysts that could offset the bear by delivering credible long-term margin targets. The honest bear position is that SNPS at $455 is more likely to decline 10-20% than to re-rate 21% as Daedalus's 12m target implies, given the near-term headwinds to FY2027 guidance and the absence of contracted evidence for the AI monetization thesis. The bear is not catastrophic -- it is a valuation argument at current prices.
